proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=97, 93.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +42.5pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8690 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=97, wr=93.7%, edge +42.5pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=97, Wilson lower 0.869, +42.5pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61 below the Tier S threshold. Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy (shifting, day 4), so the cohort is dormant; portfolio Bear exposure is 0/40 and no regime concentration is triggered (0.675 below 0.80), so promotion does not compound risk. Consistent with continuous prior approvals (2026-04-29 through 2026-07-20) and no trader rejection on record.

claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +33.3pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.6566 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.98 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
R-multiple 0.98 sits just below Tier S threshold and remains under D-8 review for Day-6 forced-exit dominance; tier may drift on next archive comparison

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=17, wr=88.2%, edge +33.3pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=17, Wilson lower 0.6566 above Tier M floor, +33.3pp edge over BULL_PROXY baseline of 54.9%) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.98 (<1.0). Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy (shifting, day 4), so it is dormant; portfolio Bear exposure is 0/40 and no regime concentration is triggered (0.675 below 0.80), so promotion does not compound concentration risk and may diversify when Bear re-emerges. Consistent with continuous prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-06-19 through 07-20) with no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_signal_type = UP_TRI (97.5%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_signal_type = UP_TRI; recommended action: Pause new UP_TRI entries until signal-type share drops below 0.85; prioritize non-UP_TRI (short/hedge/DOWN_TRI or BULL_PROXY-neutral) setups for any new adds. Consider index put hedge sized to ~20-30% of book delta given directional monoculture on unvalidated cohort.
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: A regime flip from Bull to Bear or a sharp Choppy-driven mean-reversion day hits all 40 positions simultaneously — no offsetting shorts, no validated edge cohort to justify holding through drawdown. Correlated stop-outs across sectors could compound losses since Bull×UP_TRI edge is unproven (Candidate tier, n=60).

evidence: Matched Bull×UP_TRI cohort tier = Candidate (n=60) — unvalidated edge
Regime split Bull 27 / Choppy 13 (67.5% / 32.5%) — within 0.80 tolerance but 100% directional-long exposure across both regimes
Top cell UP_TRI×Bull×Infra at 10% (within 0.20 cell threshold)
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new UP_TRI entries until signal-type share drops below 0.85; prioritize non-UP_TRI (short/hedge/DOWN_TRI or BULL_PROXY-neutral) setups for any new adds. Consider index put hedge sized to ~20-30% of book delta given directional monoculture on unvalidated cohort.
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: A regime flip from Bull to Bear or a sharp Choppy-driven mean-reversion day hits all 40 positions simultaneously — no offsetting shorts, no validated edge cohort to justify holding through drawdown. Correlated stop-outs across sectors could compound losses since Bull×UP_TRI edge is unproven (Candidate tier, n=60).
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: UP_TRI dominates signal mix at 39/40 = 97.5% (well above the 0.85 threshold), making the book a near-monoculture directional long. The matched Bull×UP_TRI cohort sits only at Candidate tier (n=60) — unvalidated edge. Regime concentration (Bull 67.5%) and the top cell (UP_TRI×Bull×Infra at 10%) are within tolerance, but signal-type monoculture on an unvalidated cohort is the actionable risk.
